question: what are the major contradictions across the document
final_answer:
## Major Contradictions Across the ICICI Bank Report

The document contains **15 identified contradictions and inconsistencies**, ranging from arithmetic errors to conflicting narrative claims. Here is a full structured breakdown:

---

## 🔴 HIGH SEVERITY — Materially Significant

### 1. ICICI Lombard Acquisition Date — Two Different Dates
| Source | Date |
|---|---|
| Subsidiary Table | February **24**, 2024 |
| Footnotes, Standalone & Consolidated Statements | February **29**, 2024 |
> A factual date cannot hold two values — one is clearly erroneous.

---

### 2. AFS Investment Total is Arithmetically Impossible (March 31, 2023)
- **AFS Gross Total — India alone:** ₹1,195,172 million
- **AFS Gross Total (India + Outside India):** ₹1,004,753.6 million *(lower than India alone — a mathematical impossibility)*

---

### 3. AFS Provision Sub-totals Don't Match the Stated Total (March 31, 2023)
- Shares (India): ₹29,712.7M + Debentures (India): ₹9,748.5M = **₹39,461.2M**
- **Stated "Total in India":** ₹18,015.9M ← A gap of ~₹21,445 million

---

### 4. "Other Investments" — Table vs. MD&A Narrative (Both Years)
| Period | Asset Table | MD&A Narrative |
|---|---|---|
| March 31, 2023 | ₹495.83 billion | ₹565.61 billion |
| March 31, 2024 | ₹752.36 billion | ₹863.68 billion |
> The discrepancy exists for **both years**, ruling out a one-time typo.

---

### 5. AIF Provision — Three Different Figures Across Chapters
| Source | Amount |
|---|---|
| Integrated Report / MD&A | ₹5.41 billion |
| Financial Statements (Standalone note) | ₹5,104.1 million (~₹5.10B) |
| Financial Statements (Consolidated note) | ₹5,105.0 million (~₹5.11B) |

---

## 🟠 MEDIUM SEVERITY — Notable Inconsistencies

### 6. HTM NPI Provisions — Disclosed Items vs. Stated Total
- Only disclosed line item: ₹54.7 million (Shares)
- Stated HTM NPI Provision for India: ₹3,773.3 million
> No other visible items bridge the ~₹3,718M gap.

---

### 7. Amazon Pay Credit Cards — "~5 Million" vs. "Over 4 Million"
| Source | Figure |
|---|---|
| Bank at a Glance section | **~5 million** cards issued till March 31, 2024 |
| Our Business Strategy section | **Over 4 million** cards issued till March 31, 2024 |

---

### 8. Scope 1 Emissions Rose, Yet Combined Scope 1+2 Claimed to Have Declined
- Scope 1 FY2023 → FY2024: 23,000 → **25,000 tCO2e** *(increased)*
- Claimed combined Scope 1+2 change: **15.7% decline** (driven entirely by a 19.7% Scope 2 reduction)
> Technically accurate in aggregate, but directionally misleading without disclosing the Scope 1 uptick.

---

### 9. Anup Bagchi — Stock Options Granted After His Departure
- **Last working day:** April 30, 2023
- **Stock options granted during fiscal 2024:** 231,000 (described as "pertaining to fiscal 2023")
> The report does not clearly explain how options were issued to a former employee post-departure.

---

### 10. Postal Ballot — "None in FY2024" vs. April 5, 2024 Notice
- Board's Report states: *"no resolution was passed through postal ballot during fiscal 2024"*
- The same section then describes a Postal Ballot Notice dated **April 5, 2024**, with resolutions passed on **May 14, 2024**

---

### 11. Net NPA Ratio — Three Different Values Across Chapters
| Source | Net NPA Ratio |
|---|---|
| Financial Statements | **0.45%** (Net NPA to Net Advances) |
| Statutory Reports / MD&A | **0.42%** (Net NPAs to Net Customer Assets) |
| Integrated Report (chart caption) | **0.4%** (rounded) |
> The 0.42% vs 0.45% difference reflects different denominators, but this distinction is **not clearly flagged** in the document.

---

## 🟡 LOW SEVERITY — Minor Discrepancies (Likely Rounding)

| # | Issue | Values |
|---|---|---|
| 12 | CSR Spend (same fiscal year) | ₹5.17B (Social Capital) vs. ₹5.19B (Business Model) |
| 13 | Profit Before Tax excl. treasury (FY2023) | ₹424.73B / 28.3% growth vs. ₹424.21B / 28.4% growth |
| 14 | Cost of Domestic Term Deposits (FY2024) | 6.21% (table) vs. 6.22% (narrative) |
| 15 | Net NPA ratio (within Integrated Report) | 0.4% (chart) vs. 0.42% (narrative) |

---

## 🔑 Key Takeaway

The **most consequential contradictions** are:
1. **Arithmetic errors** in the AFS investment tables (Items 2 & 3)
2. **ICICI Lombard acquisition date** discrepancy (Item 1)
3. The persistent **"Other Investments" mismatch** across both fiscal years (Item 4)
4. The **three-way AIF provision discrepancy** across chapters (Item 5)

These go beyond simple rounding errors and may reflect data extraction issues, OCR artifacts, or genuine inconsistencies in the source report — warranting careful verification by auditors or analysts.
